## 1\. Software Design Document

This document provides a complete overview of the COURSECORE.io web application.

## 2\. Name of those on the software team

- EHIZIBUE CHRISTIAN - \[ENG2203975\] - Frontend and DevOps
- IMARHIAGBE CALEB IYONAWAN - \[ENG2203989\] - Backend and Documentation
- OMAGE GLORY OLOLADE EGHONGHON \[ENG2204028\] - Sequence and Context Diagrams
- ARCHIBONG UKPEME MFON \[ENG2209779\] - Development process and implementation
- UDOETTE UKARA- \[ENG2204051\] - Requirements Engineering and Co-testing

## 3\. Roles of each member

- EHIZIBUE CHRISTIAN - \[ENG2203975\] - Designed and coded the entire frontend (HTML, CSS, Bootstrap), created all pages, implemented the mobile navigation toggle menu, compiled documentation (README) for consistency and clarity across projects.
- IMARHIAGBE CALEB IYONAWAN - \[ENG2203989\]- Implemented backend functionality, managed the Github repository for version control, and oversaw deployment to ensure system was operational and accessible.
- OMAGE GLORY OLOLADE EGHONGHON \[ENG2204028\]- Created sequence diagrams to illustrate system interactions and context diagrams to define system boundaries.
- ARCHIBONG UKPEME MFON \[ENG2209779\]- Applied the waterfall model to guide project phases which contributed to both frontend and backend.
- UDOETTE UKARA - \[ENG2204051\] - Documented and analyzed functional and non-functional requirements. Additionally, particicpated in testing and validation confirming the application was responsive across multiple devices.
- OSAGIE GIDEON \[ENG2204037\]- Designed UML and class diagrams to represent system architecture and relationships.
- OGIEVA GOODLUCK UWAMWENMA \[ENG2204740\] - Developed test cases to validate requirements and ensure system quality.

**4\. Version**

- SDD Version: 1.0
- Software Version: 0.1 (Frontend and Backend)

**5\. UX Requirements**

The system is designed as a **clean, modern, mobile-first interface**.

**Key UX Features:**

- Responsive design (Bootstrap 5)
- Intuitive navigation:
  - Home
  - Features
  - Reviews
  - Dashboard Login
- Fast loading (optimized static content)
- Visual clarity using icons and cards
- Minimalistic forms with placeholders

## 7\. UML Diagram

## ![](data:image/png;base64,)

## 8\. Class Diagram

## ![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Entities:**

- Student (firstName, lastName, courseOfStudy, email)
- Assignment(assignmentId, courseId, title, dueDate, status, priority)
- Dashboard(userId)
- Course (courseTitle, courseCode, difficulty, workloadRating, lecturerStyle, examStructure).
- Review (reviewId, courseId, content, rating)
- PastQuestions (questionId, courseId, upvotes, examRelevant)

## 9\. Sequence Diagram

## ![](data:image/png;base64,)

**Sequence Diagram**  
Student → System: Select course  
System → Student: Display course intelligence  
Student → System: Upload / upvote resource  
System → System: Evaluate resource  
System → Student: Assign "Exam Relevant" badge  
Student → System: Update dashboard

**10\. Implementation**

**Technologies Used:**

**Frontend**:

- HTML5 (structure)
- CSS3 + Bootstrap 5 (responsive UI)
- JavaScript (minimal interactivity)

**Backend**: FastAPI (Python framework)

**Database**: SQLite with SQLModel ORM

**Development Tools**: VS Code + Live Server(port 5500)

**11\. Source Code should be well documented**

- All major sections have comments. Example from COURSECORE.html:

**12\. Technologies Used**

- **Backend**: FastAPI (Python web framework)
- **Database**: SQLite with SQLModel ORM
- **Frontend**: HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap 5, Vanilla JavaScript
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

**13\. GitHub (Version Control)**

- Repository: GitHub (COURSECORE.io project link)
- Version control was managed using Git with clear and meaningful commit messages such as:
  - "Implement Course Intelligence UI"
  - "Add assignment tracker dashboard"
  - "Create resource upload system"
- Frontend fully pushed and viewable.

**14\. Testing, Verification and Validation stage**

- **Testing Approach**: Manual testing was carried out across all system modules.
- **Verification Activities:**
  - Ensured navigation across all pages works correctly
  - Verified Course Intelligence features display properly
  - Confirmed dashboard functionalities (assignment tracking, status updates)
  - Checked that uploaded resources display correctly
  - Verified badge assignment logic (based on upvotes and confirmations)
- **Validation Activities**
  - Tested on multiple devices: Desktop, Tablet, Mobile phones.
  - Cross-browser testing: Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge

**15\. Test framework and Report**

- Framework: Manual testing.
- Test cases passed:  
   a. Navigation menu opens and closes correctly on mobile devices  
   b. Course intelligence data displays accurately  
   c. Assignment tracker updates status correctly (Not Started / In Progress / Done)

d. Resource upload and display functionality works  
e. Verified badge ("Exam Relevant") is assigned correctly based on conditions  
f. All pages maintain responsiveness across devices

- **Test Report**:

All system components function as expected.  
The modular structure of COURSECORE.io ensured easy testing and debugging.  
The defined scope allowed for complete verification without c

**16\. SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT PROCESS MODELS**

**WATERFALL MODEL**

This project involves the development of **COURSECORE.io**, an academic support platform for Computer Engineering students. The **Waterfall model** was selected based on clearly defined requirements and structured system functionality.

**Why Waterfall?**

- All core features (Course Intelligence + Dashboard + Resource System) were defined at the beginning.
- Suitable for academic projects requiring detailed documentation.
- Provides a clear and sequential development process

**Phases Applied**

- Requirements Analysis
- System Design
- Implementation
- Testing
- Deployment and Maintenance

**WATERFALL MODEL ADAPTED FOR COURSECORE.io**

**REQUIREMENT ANALYSIS PHASE**

**Activities:**

- Defined Course Intelligence features
- Designed Academic Dashboard structure.
- Specified resource upload and verification system
- Identified user interactions and workflows

**Stakeholders:**

- Students (Primary Users)
- Admin (System Management)

**Functional Requirements:**

The system shall:

- Display a **Course Intelligence Layer**, including:
  - Course difficulty insights
  - Workload ratings
  - Lecturer style tags
  - Exam structure breakdown
  - Anonymous student reviews
  - Past question uploads
  - Tagging and filtering system
  - Course comparison tool
- Provide a **Personal Academic Dashboard**, including:
  - Assignment tracker
  - Deadline calendar
  - Progress tracking (Not Started / In Progress / Done)
  - Course-linked assignments
  - Priority tagging
  - Reminder system
- Implement a **Verified Resource Badge System**:
  - Assign ✔️ _"Exam Relevant" badge_ when:
    - A resource is highly upvoted
    - Confirmed by multiple students

**Non Functional Requirements:**

- Responsive design
- Fast loading performance
- User-friendly interface
- System reliability and consistency
- Scalable architecture for future upgrades

**IMPLEMENTATION PHASE**

- Developed frontend interfaces using HTML, CSS, and Bootstrap.
- Implemented backend APIs using FastAPI.
- Integrated database using SQLite and SQLModel.
- Built system modules incrementally:
  - Course Intelligence Layer
  - Dashboard
  - Resource system
- Maintained regular Git commits for version tracking

**TESTING PHASE**

- Conducted manual testing on all modules.
- Verified:
  - UI responsiveness
  - Feature functionality
  - Badge logic
- Fixed all identified issues immediately

**DEPLOYMENT PHASE**

- Local deployment via VS Code Live Server.
- Backend tested via local FastAPI server

MAINTENANCE

- Integration of real-time database
- User authentication system
- Notification system improvements
- AI-based course recommendations

**Advantages of Waterfall Model**

- Clear structure and milestones
- Easy documentation and reporting
- Suitable for well-defined systems like COURSECORE.io

&nbsp;Clear milestones and excellent documentation.

**Limitations**

- Difficult to accommodate changes after implementation
- Less flexible for evolving requirements

**Conclusion**

The Waterfall model ensured that **COURSECORE.io** was developed in a structured and organized manner, resulting in a **clean, functional, and well-documented academic platform** tailored to student needs.
